# Oh My Experience

Oh My Experience is a local-first experience layer for AI coding agents. It turns real Codex and Claude work into approved experience cards, then can recall relevant approved lessons at prompt time through local hooks.

## Start here

- Overview: /
- Quickstart: /guides/quickstart
- First experience card: /guides/first-card
- Examples: /guides/examples
- Setup: /guides/setup
- Source scan: /guides/source-scan
- Global and project libraries: /guides/project-libraries
- Codex hook: /guides/codex
- Claude hook: /guides/claude

## Core concepts

- Experience card lifecycle: candidate -> draft -> active -> archived.
- `dataDir` is the global library; optional project libraries live at `<project-root>/.oh-my-experience/`.
- Global cards can still be scoped by project metadata without adding files to a repository.
- Prompt-time recall happens through Codex and Claude UserPromptSubmit hooks.
- Deep retrospectives should build a temporary Codex/Claude user-only evidence index before Agent query expansion and context replay.
- During retrospectives, matched subject-area cards are overlap signals, not source evidence or final used-card disclosures.
- Before adding a new card, compare it with active cards and record candidate and adjacent-card actions separately.
- Hook frames and fixed labels are English; approved card content targets English or Chinese; source evidence may keep its original language.
- User-visible OME recall reminders and used-card disclosures rely on the agent's normal response-language behavior.
- Retrospectives create candidates; they do not directly create active cards.
- Draft approval stays Markdown-first; candidates do not become active without explicit enabling.
- OME is local-first. Hook runtime does not call an LLM.

- CLI: /reference/cli
